[[BLOCK:slide34_shape1]]
Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC) imposes a heavier penalty. However, AIC and AICc are considered superior to BIC. AIC and AICc are based on the principle of information gain. The Bayesian approach requires prior input, which is often subject to debate. AIC is asymptotically optimal for model selection in terms of minimizing the least squared mean error, whereas BIC is not asymptotically optimal (Burnham & Anderson, 2004; Yang, 2005).
